problem_statement: Traditional code review processes and static analysis tools lack the depth to provide actionable feedback beyond syntax errors or known bug patterns; there is no LLM-based tool that conducts detailed reviews, detects bugs and code smells, and educates developers on best practices.
magnitude_evidence: Thongtanunam et al. [16] discovered that 30% of code reviews face challenges with assigning the correct reviewers
insufficiency_evidence: Traditional code review processes and static analysis tools, often lack the depth to provide actionable feedback beyond the detection of syntax errors or known patterns of bugs; in several cases it detected issues that traditional static analysis tools either missed or reported with very limited explanation
solution_mechanisms: Code Review Agent for initial issue detection
Bug Report Agent for bug identification
Code Smell Agent for refactoring suggestions
centralized coordination component for inter-agent messaging
notes: Short early-results/position paper; no quantitative evaluation of the proposed system reported (only qualitative preliminary observations).
